title: triodia mutica
intro: Triodia mutica, also known as spear grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Australia, characterized by its sharp, pointed leaves that can cause injury if not handled carefully. The plant has been used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Triodia mutica include its ability to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Triodia mutica can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and poultices, which are often used in traditional Aboriginal medicine to promote healing and well-being.
